executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

National Medicaid enrollment continues its post-pandemic normalization trajectory, with 74.9 million enrollees across 51 reporting states as of February 2026. All major states are experiencing enrollment declines as redetermination processes conclude, with year-over-year decreases ranging from minimal in North Carolina (0.0%) to substantial in Michigan (-7.6%). Texas enrollment declined 3.8% to approximately 4.0 million beneficiaries, positioning it as the third-largest state program behind California and New York. National spending totaled $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, yielding a per-enrollee average of $2,883, with significant state variation reflecting different population demographics, benefit packages, and cost structures.

Texas demonstrates mixed performance relative to peer states across key metrics. With per-enrollee costs of $3,014, Texas falls near the national average and compares favorably to higher-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Ohio ($3,584), while operating above lower-cost states like Florida ($2,036) and North Carolina ($2,623). However, Texas quality performance presents improvement opportunities, scoring 62 out of 100 and ranking 30th nationally—trailing regional peers and falling into the "moderate" performance tier. The state's managed care penetration rate of 93.3% significantly exceeds the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong care coordination infrastructure that should theoretically support better outcomes.

Recent federal regulatory activity signals heightened scrutiny around state-directed payments, provider enrollment moratoria, and community engagement requirements, areas that may impact state program operations. Given Texas's substantial managed care investment but middling quality outcomes, leadership should prioritize targeted quality improvement initiatives and consider leveraging the state's robust managed care platform to drive measurable performance gains in the upcoming measurement period.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.0 million enrollees, representing a substantial 5.3% of national enrollment despite serving only 13.7% of its state population—well below peer states like California (31.6%) and New York (32.9%). The state's enrollment declined 3.8% year-over-year, tracking closely with national trends as states complete post-pandemic eligibility redeterminations. At $3,014 per enrollee, Texas spends 4.5% above the national average of $2,883, positioning it between low-cost Florida ($2,036) and high-cost expansion states like Pennsylvania ($4,363). This spending level reflects Texas's status as a non-expansion state with a narrower eligibility base, requiring higher per-capita investments in acute care for its predominantly children and pregnant women population.

Texas demonstrates operational strengths in managed care implementation, with 93.3% penetration significantly exceeding the national average of 83.2%, indicating sophisticated care coordination infrastructure. However, quality outcomes remain a critical challenge, with Texas scoring 62/100—below the national average of 65.6 and ranking 30th nationally. This places Texas in "moderate" performance territory, ahead of southern peers like South Carolina (53) and Alabama (52), but trailing North Carolina (estimated ~66 based on per-enrollee spending patterns). Key areas to monitor include ongoing federal regulatory changes around community engagement requirements and state-directed payments, both visible in recent Federal Register activity, which could impact Texas's managed care model. The state's relatively efficient spending combined with strong managed care penetration suggests potential for quality improvements through targeted interventions, particularly given its large scale provides leverage for system-wide reforms.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Has Extremely High Per-Enrollee Spending
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 is 51% above the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost inefficiencies or unique population needs.

alerts.title: Massachusetts High Cost Despite Top Quality Performance
alerts.description: Massachusetts has the highest per-enrollee spending at $4,221 (46% above national average) but also leads in quality scores at 81/100, suggesting high-value care delivery.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Poor Quality Performance
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55/100 on quality measures, significantly below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Florida Shows Low Spending But Average Quality Concerns
alerts.description: Florida has the lowest per-enrollee spending at $2,036 (29% below national average) but lacks corresponding quality data, raising questions about care adequacy.

alerts.title: Nationwide Provider Enrollment Moratorium Active
alerts.description: Federal Register shows temporary moratoria on Medicare/Medicaid provider enrollment, which could impact access to care across all states.

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score below national average at #30 ranking
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scored 62/100 on quality measures, ranking #30 of 50 states and falling below the national average of 65.6. This places Texas in the moderate tier, well below high performers like MA (81) and MN (80).
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Quality performance directly impacts contract renewals and federal oversight. HHSC may face pressure to improve outcomes, creating opportunities for Accenture to propose quality improvement initiatives.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement opportunities to HHSC leadership

executiveInsights.title: Texas 93.3% managed care penetration exceeds national 83.2% average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3.86M MC enrollees, significantly higher than the national average of 83.2%. This represents strong managed care adoption in the state.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration indicates sophisticated contract management needs and potential for advanced analytics and automation solutions that Accenture can provide.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review managed care optimization opportunities with HHSC

executiveInsights.title: Texas enrollment declined 3.8% YoY to 4M enrollees
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.8% year-over-year to 3,997,752 enrollees, consistent with national unwinding trends. The decline aligns with the national pattern as states resume eligibility redeterminations.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment declines may impact contract scope and revenue projections. Accenture should adjust resource planning and identify efficiency opportunities as caseloads normalize.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review staffing models and contract scope adjustments with HHSC

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs at $3,014 above national average of $2,883
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $3,014 per enrollee compared to the national average of $2,883, representing a 4.5% premium. Total Texas Medicaid expenditures reached $12.05 billion in fiscal 2024.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-enrollee costs may indicate opportunities for cost management initiatives and could signal budget pressure that affects contract negotiations and scope.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag cost optimization strategies to HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: Recent Federal Register shows new community engagement requirements
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register published new community engagement requirements for certain Medicaid individuals on June 3, 2026. This represents a significant policy change affecting program administration.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: New federal requirements will likely require system changes, policy updates, and operational adjustments that create implementation opportunities for Accenture services.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review community engagement rule impacts with HHSC policy team

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline acceleration
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.8% YoY, risking reduced contract values and operational scale.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop cost optimization strategies and diversify revenue streams beyond enrollment-based contracts.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality performance gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, creating improvement mandate pressure.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position Accenture as quality improvement partner with proven methodologies to reach top-tier performance.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: High 93.3% managed care penetration signals potential major procurement cycles affecting $11B+ in spending.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor HHSC procurement portal and prepare comprehensive managed care modernization proposals.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory shifts
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple 2026 Federal Register entries indicate significant Medicaid program changes requiring state adaptation.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory tracking team and offer compliance consulting services to HHSC.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG oversight intensification
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG Medicaid portfolio suggests heightened scrutiny and potential audit risks for contractors.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen compliance frameworks and position as trusted partner for audit preparation and response.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost efficiency optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee spending at $3,014 vs national $2,883 average presents cost reduction opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop data-driven cost management solutions targeting administrative efficiency and fraud prevention.

riskOpportunity.label: Digital transformation mandate
riskOpportunity.description: Community engagement and data collection requirements create technology modernization needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose integrated digital platforms addressing new federal requirements and member engagement.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive positioning risk
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple states showing similar challenges may intensify consultant competition for limited resources.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Differentiate through Texas-specific expertise and proven outcomes in similar large-state environments.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 3,997,752 Medicaid enrollees as of February 2026.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas Medicaid enrollment decreased by 3.8% year-over-year.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $3,014 per Medicaid enrollee annually in fiscal year 2024.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas ranks #30 of 50 states with a quality score of 62 out of 100 in 2024.
